name: Menasalbas-Necropolis Trail (PR-TO 36)
name_original: Embalse del Torcón desde Menasalbas — circular por la Ruta Menasalbas-Necrópolis (PR-TO 36)
description: Hike the moderate 11.2-mile Embalse del Torcón circular route, exploring ancient necropolises and enjoying reservoir views near Menasalbas.
region_intro: Hiking around Menasalbas, a municipality in Toledo, Spain, offers routes through varied terrain. The landscape is characterized by rolling hills, woodlands, and open countryside, with moderate elevation changes. Trails often traverse agricultural areas and natural spaces, providing views of the local environment. The region's geology includes features like ravines and elevated points such as La Morra and Peña Gorda. Best hiking trails around Menasalbas The most popular hiking route is Hoz de Carboneros Trail, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores a ravine area with natural features. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Morra and El Cerillón Loop, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.7 km) path. This trail ascends to elevated points, offering panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Las Majadillas and Peña Gorda Loop, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail leading through hills and open areas, often completed in about 2 hours 43 minutes. Hiking around Menasalbas is defined by rolling hills, woodlands, and open countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to longer excursions. The routes in Menasalbas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Menasalbas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Menasalbas?
A: There are over 30 hiking routes in the Menasalbas area, offering a variety of experiences through rolling hills, woodlands, and open countryside. These include 6 easy routes, 18 moderate routes, and 6 difficult routes.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Menasalbas?
A: Yes, Menasalbas offers 6 easy hiking trails. While specific names aren't listed as easy, the region's landscape of rolling hills and moderate elevation changes generally provides accessible options for those new to hiking or looking for a relaxed walk.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes in Menasalbas?
A: The hiking trails around Menasalbas often traverse varied terrain, including woodlands, open countryside, and agricultural areas. You can expect to encounter geological features like ravines, and elevated points such as La Morra and Peña Gorda, which offer panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular walks in the Menasalbas area?
A: Yes, many routes in Menasalbas are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1020945007/la-morra-and-el-cerillon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morra and El Cerillón Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate path that ascends to elevated points, providing scenic views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1198274442/las-majadillas-and-pena-gorda-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Majadillas and Peña Gorda Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through hills and open areas.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Menasalbas?
A: The hiking experiences in Menasalbas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from woodlands to open countryside, and the panoramic views offered from elevated points like La Morra and Peña Gorda.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to explore near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the region around Menasalbas has several points of interest. You could visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5415856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Melque Complex&lt;/a&gt;, a significant historical site, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menasalbas Village Center&lt;/a&gt; itself. For those interested in religious heritage, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5636457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of the Antigua&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or viewpoints in the area?
A: Hikers can discover several natural attractions and viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estena Towers&lt;/a&gt; offer an interesting natural monument, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5421163" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerillón&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5421153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morra&lt;/a&gt; are popular summits and viewpoints providing expansive vistas of the landscape.

Q: Is there a trail that offers a good overview of the region's geology?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1196873647/hoz-de-carboneros-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz de Carboneros Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.5 km route that specifically explores a ravine area, showcasing some of the region's natural geological features. It's a good option for understanding the local terrain.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes in Menasalbas?
A: Yes, for those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1198355508/menasalbas-necropolis-trail-pr-to-36" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menasalbas-Necropolis Trail (PR-TO 36)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 18.1 km route. Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1020949286/la-morra-and-cerillon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morra and Cerillón Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 14.6 km and offers significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Menasalbas?
A: Menasalbas experiences varied weather. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winters are typically mild but can be damp.

Q: Is parking readily available near the trailheads in Menasalbas?
A: While specific parking areas for each trailhead are not detailed, Menasalbas is a rural municipality. Parking is generally available in or near the village center, and at various points along the main roads leading to popular hiking areas. It's advisable to check local maps or komoot tour details for specific starting points.
tips: A nice path along the shore of the reservoir. Keep in mind that if it has rained a lot, the path may be covered in water and you will have to go a little higher.
Temple of the S. XVI faithful to the rural Gothic style of the Montes de Toledo.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: The Embalse del Torcón from Menasalbas — circular via the Menasalbas-Necrópolis Route (PR-TO 36) is approximately 18.1 km (11.2 miles) long and generally takes around 5 hours and 45 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While it doesn't present extreme technical challenges, its length is the primary factor contributing to this rating. There are few significant elevations, with the main climb being to Cabeza del Torcón, which is not overly demanding.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Menasalbas-Necrópolis Route?
faqs.answer: You can expect varied terrain, including tracks, paths, trails, and some off-road sections. The route traverses a predominantly livestock farming area and features numerous granite rock formations. A significant portion runs alongside the Torcón Reservoir.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail is rich in historical and natural features. You'll encounter the ancient Necrópolis del Torcón with its anthropomorphic tombs, unusual granite rock formations known as Piedras Caballeras (including the 'Toro del Torcón'), and sections of the historic Cañada Real Segoviana. The route also offers panoramic views from Cabeza del Torcón and scenic beauty around the Torcón Reservoir. You might also pass the Hermitage of Our Lady of Health, Fuente de las Cancheras, and the Villapalos Bridge and Mill.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is particularly recommended during the spring and winter seasons. Spring offers pleasant temperatures and blooming Mediterranean flora, while winter provides crisp air and unique landscapes. Summers can be very hot, so it's best to avoid peak summer months or hike very early in the morning.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse del Torcón from Menasalbas — circular via the Menasalbas-Necrópolis Route (PR-TO 36) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is generally dog-friendly. However, as it traverses a predominantly livestock farming area, it's crucial to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing near cattle, sheep, or other farm animals, to ensure their safety and respect local wildlife and livestock.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this hike?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details are not provided, the route starts from Menasalbas. It's advisable to look for designated parking areas within or on the outskirts of Menasalbas that offer convenient access to the PR-TO 36 trailhead.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Embalse del Torcón from Menasalbas — circular via the Menasalbas-Necrópolis Route (PR-TO 36). It is a public trail accessible to all.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and flora can I observe?
faqs.answer: The trail boasts diverse Mediterranean flora, including almond trees, holm oaks, and rockroses. You might also spot various wildlife such as cattle, sheep, pigs, horses, eagles, and rabbits, especially in the livestock farming areas and natural landscapes.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, it covers 96% of the 'Ruta Mensalbas - Necrópolis' and also intersects with parts of the 'Camino de los Montes de Toledo - Etapa 3' and the historic 'Cañada Real Segoviana'.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or pubs near the trailhead in Menasalbas?
faqs.answer: Menasalbas is a local town, so you can expect to find cafes, restaurants, and pubs there where you can refresh yourself before or after your hike. It's a good idea to check local opening hours, especially if you're hiking on a weekend or public holiday.
